title: Accept spoiler_text and sensitive params in POST /api/v1/statuses
description:
## What
Accept `spoiler_text` and `sensitive` parameters in POST /api/v1/statuses; validate spoiler_text length <= max_post_chars; pass both through to post creation logic.

## Why
Without accepting these params, clients cannot post content warnings — a core Mastodon feature used for sensitive or spoiler content.

## Agent team pattern
- Spawn a Research subagent to: review the Mastodon POST /api/v1/statuses API docs and curl mastodon.social to confirm accepted params, validation errors returned for oversized spoiler_text, and how sensitive interacts with media attachments
- Spawn an Implementation subagent to: update the POST /api/v1/statuses handler to parse spoiler_text and sensitive from request body, validate spoiler_text length, and persist both to the posts table
- Spawn a Test subagent to: write tests covering (a) post with valid spoiler_text round-trips correctly, (b) spoiler_text exceeding max_post_chars returns 422, (c) sensitive=true is stored and returned

## Test discipline
- Independent oracle: mastodon.social /api/v1/statuses POST behavior — oversized spoiler_text returns 422 with error field
- Test approach: parameterized tests with boundary values for spoiler_text length; assert HTTP status codes match Mastodon reference behavior

## Defensive programming checklist
- No `.unwrap()` in handler paths
- Treat spoiler_text as untrusted input — HTML-escape on read (serialization), store raw in DB
- Validate spoiler_text byte length against same max_post_chars limit as post content
- Return 422 Unprocessable Entity for validation failures, matching Mastodon behavior
